lax_friedrichs_flux.reference: Lax & Friedrichs (1954), Comm. Pure Appl. Math. 7(1):159-193 — original LF flux. For linear advection f(q) = c·q with face-staggered Courant c = u·dt/dx, the LF numerical flux F_{i+1/2} = c·(q_i + q_{i+1})/2 - |c|/2·(q_{i+1} - q_i) reduces to first-order upwinding: F_{i+1/2} = max(c,0)·q_i + min(c,0)·q_{i+1}. Reference imperative implementation in src/operators/flux_1d.jl (CubedSphereGrid wrapper); the Cartesian core encoded here matches that computation in-panel.

flux_form.comment: Algebraic identity: F_{i+1/2} = (c+|c|)/2·q_i + (c-|c|)/2·q_{i+1} = max(c,0)·q_i + min(c,0)·q_{i+1}. Upwind cell selection is encoded directly in the abs op — no caller-side branching on sign(c) is required.

lax_friedrichs_flux.composition_hint: Caller computes the face-staggered Courant c_{i+1/2} = u_{i+1/2}·dt/dx and applies this rule at every interface; the cell-centred tendency follows from the FV divergence T_i = -(F_{i+1/2} - F_{i-1/2})/h on a uniform grid (or composes with divergence_arakawa_c on a C-grid). Reduces to first-order upwinding: dissipative by construction, retained as a debugging / oracle scheme — production transport should use the higher-order PPM or WENO rules.
lax_friedrichs_flux.schema_notes: The face-Courant `$c` is a per-face binding supplied by the caller (analogous to the slope-ratio `$r` in flux_limiter_minmod), not a stencil-offset selector. Grid-family-agnostic in practice — the Cartesian declaration here follows the §7 convention; an axis-agnostic / multi-grid-family generalisation will compose with future face-stagger schema features (boundary_policy + time-varying bindings; tracked as follow-up beads off dsc-35x).
limitations: Cubed-sphere wrapper (src/operators/flux_1d.jl): in-panel coefficients match the Cartesian core verbatim, but cross-panel ghost extension and panel-boundary distance handling (grid.dist_xi_bnd / grid.dist_eta_bnd) live in the cubed_sphere grid accessor and are not yet expressible in the rule because the cubed_sphere selector kind has no face-stagger slot. Tracked as follow-up dependency on schema beads grid_dispatch + boundary_policy.

PPM-based flux-form transport (flux_1d_ppm! and flux_1d_ppm_arrayop in src/operators/flux_1d.jl) is a separate higher-order scheme not encoded here; tracked as a follow-up bead pending time-varying bindings + boundary_policy schema features.
